---
title: "About"
---

This is the companion site for a research project on **Causal SHAP for target
DAGs** — an open, reproducible study of which attribution value function recovers
useful upstream intervention targets, and which merely rewards features near the
outcome.

## Authors

Andy Wilson. See the repository
[CITATION.cff](https://github.com/andystats/causal-shap-target-dags/blob/main/CITATION.cff)
for the canonical citation.

## Data provenance

Every dataset on this site is **synthetic**.

- **NASA renal-stone (flagship).** The DAG topology is source-exact, parsed from
  the public NASA DAGitty source (SA-07566), and validated against it (Cohen's κ =
  1.0). All coefficients are simulation parameters, **not** NASA estimates.
- **Teaching DAGs (toy, ladder).** Hand-built linear-Gaussian structural models
  designed so that a proxy is predictive yet has zero total effect.
- **ACIC proxy stress test.** A designed many-mediator DAG where five proxies are
  predictive with zero effect by construction.

## Method background

- Heskes, Sijben, Bucur, Claassen (2020). *Causal Shapley Values.* NeurIPS.
- Frye, Rowat, Feige (2020). *Asymmetric Shapley Values.* NeurIPS.
- Janzing, Minorics, Blöbaum (2020). *Feature Relevance Quantification in
  Explainable AI: A Causal Problem.* AISTATS.
- Parikh, Varjão, Xu, Tchetgen Tchetgen (2022). *Validating Causal Inference
  Methods* (Credence). ICML.
- Instats workshop *Modern Analysis of Real-World Data* (Wilson, Gregg, Morrison),
  from which the DAG vocabulary, discovery playground, and Credence-style
  validation are adapted.

The simulation-validation layer is implemented from the author's own workshop code
in the spirit of Credence; no code from the Credence repository is redistributed.

## Provisional components

The complexity score (**PSCI v0**) is an explicitly provisional placeholder. It
plugs into a small registry seam so the authors' final score can replace it
without touching the rest of the workflow.

## License

Code is released under the MIT License. This site's prose and figures accompany the
paper; please cite the repository if you build on them.
